## 3. Deep Research panel

### Lifetime (83 reviewed out of 125 BUY/BUY_LIMIT = **66.4% DR coverage of green verdicts**; 16.2% of all 511 decisions)

| DR review verdict | Count | Rate (of 83 reviewed) |
|---|---:|---:|
| ADJUSTED | 45 | 54.2% |
| OVERRIDDEN | 32 | 38.6% |
| ERROR_PARSING | 3 | 3.6% |
| UPGRADED | 1 | 1.2% |
| INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS | 1 | 1.2% |
| CONFIRMED | 1 | 1.2% |

DR continues to *fight the council* heavily: **92.8% of reviewed DR cases either adjusted or overrode the PM verdict**, with a 38.6% straight-up OVERRIDE rate. Only one explicit "CONFIRMED" in the lifetime. **DR coverage gap is 43 of 125 BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows (34.4%) — see §6.HIGH-3.**

### Today's individual DR cases

**None.** Zero BUY / BUY_LIMIT verdicts today, so DR had nothing to review. The DR worker queue should be idle.

**Carry-forward from yesterday — DR did not execute on ARGTF (BUY_LIMIT recorded 2026-05-18 14:15 ET).** Yesterday's audit warned: "if ARGTF stays NULL into tomorrow's run, escalate." As of this run (2026-05-19 15:09 ET, **~25 hours after PM verdict**), `deep_research_review_verdict` is still NULL. This is a **HIGH-severity DR funnel gap** — the worker is either dead, deadlocked, or has silently dropped the request. See §6.HIGH-4.

| ACM | 2026-05-12 | 69.95 | 71.26 | +1.87% | 65.80 | 79.27 | +7.66% | green |

🔴 **HIGH — WTW stop-loss anomaly.** The recorded `stop_loss_price` of 253.50 is **above** both the entry price (248.99) and the current price (247.72). The position is, by the table convention used everywhere else, **already past its stop loss by 2.33%** — yet the position is still ACTIVE and the desk has not flagged it (`last_reviewed_at` = 2026-05-14T19:36). Either (a) the SL field is being abused as a trailing ceiling/breakeven mark and the column name lies, or (b) the stop event simply did not fire. Either way, this needs human eyes before the next scan window. See §6.HIGH-2.

CART headroom is positive but tight; if today's MSCI/Asia-ADR weakness continues into tomorrow it could touch 37.95.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

### 🔴 HIGH

- **HIGH-1 · `decision_tracking` is still empty (0 rows).** The post-decision price tracker has never written a row across 511 lifetime decisions. This kills any retrospective accuracy analysis, undermines the deep-research feedback loop, and makes the backtest backlog item (#1) impossible. Unchanged from prior audits — *not improving on its own*.
- **HIGH-2 · WTW stop-loss inversion (id=28).** `stop_loss_price = 253.50` is above `entry_price = 248.99` and above `current_price = 247.72`. By the convention every other ACTIVE/CLOSED row follows (SL below entry for longs), this position should have been closed. `last_reviewed_at = 2026-05-14T19:36` — no review in 5 days. Either a column-misuse bug or a missed stop event.
- **HIGH-3 · DR funnel coverage gap — 43/125 BUY/BUY_LIMIT (34.4%) have no DR verdict.** Most recent uncovered rows: ARGTF (2026-05-18), APPF (2026-05-13), APP (2026-05-13), ELAN (2026-05-11), VIV (2026-05-11), FLUT (2026-05-11), NTRA (2026-05-08), GMAB (2026-05-08), CRWV (2026-05-08), CHYM (2026-05-07), CLH (2026-05-06), CDW (2026-05-06), SHOP (2026-05-05), PCOR (2026-05-05), ETN (2026-05-05), AEIS (2026-05-05). Several became live `Owned` positions without DR ever reviewing them.
- **HIGH-4 · ARGTF DR worker stall (escalation from 2026-05-18 audit).** ARGTF was flagged as a single fresh BUY_LIMIT yesterday with a rate-limited DR worker explanation. **~25 hours later it is still NULL** — that exceeds any legitimate rate-limit explanation. The DR worker thread is likely dead or the queue is dropping enqueues silently.
- **HIGH-5 · `sector` column NULL on 504/511 rows (98.6%).** Only 7 rows ever got a sector populated. The entire screening-haul §5 of this report has to bucket clusters by company-name inference instead of the DB field. Likely a regression in the screener's enrichment step.
- **HIGH-6 · `subscribers` table empty.** The daily email goes nowhere. If the email service is supposed to be live, this is silent total failure; if it's intentionally disabled, the background `email_service` task is wasting cycles.
- **HIGH-7 · Test rows in production tables.** `decision_points` contains 9 fake symbols inserted on 2026-05-09: TEST, TEST_T3/T4/T5/T12, T8_BUY, T8_BUY_LIMIT, T8_WATCH, T8_AVOID. Five of them are marked `status='Owned'`. They pollute every aggregate query and three (TEST_T3, TEST_T12, TEST) appear in the "Owned-without-active-position" set in §6.MEDIUM-1.

### 🟠 MEDIUM

- **MEDIUM-1 · 103 `decision_points` with `status='Owned'` but no ACTIVE `desk_positions` row.** Most are stale historical entries where the desk_position has since closed — only a problem if it confuses the views layer. The reconcile job (if any) is not running.
- **MEDIUM-2 · MHVIY status mismatch.** `desk_positions.status='ACTIVE'` but the linked `decision_points.status='Not Owned'`. Inverse of MEDIUM-1 and far more dangerous — the dashboard may not show MHVIY as owned even though it is.
- **MEDIUM-3 · Stuck PENDING decision_points >24h old: 28 rows.** Includes today's still-pending HUT (legitimately fresh), 4 T8_* test rows (junk), and a tail back to 2026-04-09 (APA). The pipeline never returns to clean these up.
- **MEDIUM-4 · `ai_score` NULL on 501/511 rows (98.0%).** All 125 BUY/BUY_LIMIT rows have NULL `ai_score`. The field is being defined and migrated but never populated.
- **MEDIUM-5 · `desk_reviews` last activity = 2026-05-14 (5 days dark).** No review records since the last batch of FLR/SHOP exits. Active positions are aging without re-evaluation — the Sell Council (`reassess_positions.py`) is either off or its writes aren't landing.
- **MEDIUM-6 · 3 ERROR_PARSING DR rows (HQY 2026-04-10, PPERF 2026-04-11, ADBE 2026-04-23).** Older parser failures still on the books — no follow-up.

### 🟡 LOW

- **LOW-1 · Today's volume 14 vs 14-day baseline 18.3.** Modest under-shoot; well within variance and consistent with a quiet pre-Memorial-Day Tuesday.
- **LOW-2 · `batch_comparisons` last activity = 2026-05-08.** Eleven days dark. Either the batch flow is intentionally throttled or it's idle.
- **LOW-3 · `git_version` NULL on 9 rows in May.** Cosmetic, but suggests the version-stamping decorator isn't covering every code path.

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The book is frozen.** Six straight sessions (since 2026-05-14) with no entries and no exits. The 5 ACTIVE positions are aging in place, with WTW the standout problem (§6.HIGH-2). Risk surface is concentrated in stale positions while the screener keeps producing AVOIDs.
- **PM has correctly read the regime shift.** AVOID share has run 64.3% today and 66.7% yesterday vs 51.5% lifetime. The crypto-miner / AI-power / Asian-exporter cluster on offer today is exactly the basket DR has been overriding to AVOID for the past 8 trading sessions (CEG, AXON, J, NGG, KGC). PM internalised the signal.
- **DR is the highest-value step in the funnel.** Override rate 38.6% with adjustments on top brings PM-modification rate to 92.8%. PPERY (ex-div mechanical), J (debt), CEG (rate shock), GAP (settlement-as-catalyst illusion) are textbook saves. The system would carry materially worse trades without it.
- **DR coverage is the highest-value bug.** A third of BUY/BUY_LIMITs never see DR — and several of those became live positions (CRWV, NTRA, GMAB, SHOP, ETN, AEIS, PCOR, CDW, CLH, CART itself). If DR is catching real failures on the trades it does see, the untreated 34% is leaking alpha.
- **Closed-position P&L is healthier than it looks at a glance.** TAKE_PROFIT exits avg +6.28% on 15 trades; STOP_LOSS exits avg −5.77% on 6. The drag is DESK_DECISION exits (18 trades, avg −0.36%) — a time-out class that is net-flat but consuming most of the position slots. Worth instrumenting *why* the desk is exiting these manually before TP/SL fires.
- **The "feedback-loop" subsystem is structurally inactive.** `decision_tracking=0`, `ai_score=NULL` on virtually every row, `subscribers=0`, `desk_reviews` 5 days dark. Each of these alone would be a regression; together they describe a write-only pipeline.
- **Small-sample noise to ignore.** Today's 14-row haul, the single OVERRIDDEN-vs-CONFIRMED gap (1 lifetime CONFIRMED is barely a category), and the LOW-2 batch idle window are all within plausible quiet-week variance — don't react to any of them in isolation.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions (prioritised by impact)

1. **(HIGH) Reconcile WTW immediately.** Pull the position into a manual review — decide whether (a) the SL field has been repurposed as a different stop concept and needs renaming, or (b) the stop event misfired and the position should have closed. Until resolved, treat WTW as an unmodelled risk.
2. **(HIGH) Restart / debug the DR worker and backfill ARGTF.** Check whether the daemon thread is alive (`pgrep`/log inspection on the `deep_research_service` worker). Re-enqueue ARGTF manually so the DR coverage stays auditable.
3. **(HIGH) Drive DR coverage to 100% of BUY/BUY_LIMIT.** Add a guard in `research_service` that refuses to ship a BUY/BUY_LIMIT downstream without an associated DR verdict (allowing ERROR_PARSING but not NULL). Backfill the existing 43 gap rows in priority order: still-`Owned` first.
4. **(HIGH) Wire up `decision_tracking` writes.** This unlocks backtesting, the feedback loop, and the eval window extension (improvement-backlog items #1–#3). Without it, every retrospective claim in this report is structurally unverifiable.
5. **(HIGH) Fix sector enrichment.** 98.6% NULL is not a transient outage — likely a path that was disabled or a dependency that broke. Restoring it makes the cluster analysis in §5 first-class and unblocks any sector-aware filtering.
6. **(HIGH) Purge test rows from `decision_points`.** The 9 TEST/T8 rows from 2026-05-09 distort every aggregate (`status='Owned'` counts, BUY-without-DR counts, etc.). Delete or move to a `decision_points_test` table.
7. **(MEDIUM) Reconcile `desk_positions` ↔ `decision_points.status`.** MHVIY's inverse mismatch (MEDIUM-2) is the dangerous case. Add a nightly job that diffs the two and writes corrections.
8. **(MEDIUM) Run a Sell Council pass on the 5 active positions.** `desk_reviews` is 5 days stale; WTW, CART, MSI, MHVIY, ACM should all be re-scored at least every 2 trading sessions while the book is otherwise frozen.
9. **(MEDIUM) Either populate `subscribers` (start with sim.j.baum@gmail.com) or short-circuit the email task.** Silent failure is the worst state.
10. **(MEDIUM) Investigate the FJIKY data-gap path.** Japanese ADRs keep falling out at PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA. Either widen the resolver or document the cohort as un-screenable.
11. **(MEDIUM) Audit the DESK_DECISION exit class.** 18 net-flat exits is a lot of capacity to spend on time-outs; instrument *why* the desk is exiting and whether tighter TP/SL or a shorter `max_hold_days` would harvest more of the underlying mean-reversion.
12. **(LOW) Stamp `git_version` on every code path** and prune the stuck-PENDING backlog (28 rows, mostly historical).
